body.processed:
Tax preparation is about more than filling out forms. Our CPAs take a comprehensive approach to your individual tax return, ensuring you take advantage of every deduction and credit available to you while maintaining full compliance with federal and state tax laws.

What We Handle

  • Federal and state income tax returns
  • Investment income and capital gains reporting
  • Rental property income and expenses
  • Self-employment and freelance income (Schedule C)
  • Stock options and equity compensation
  • Foreign income and expat tax returns
  • Multi-state tax filings
  • IRS audit representation and correspondence

Our Process

We make tax preparation as painless as possible:

  • Organize: We provide a personalized checklist of documents you'll need, based on your specific situation
  • Review: Your CPA reviews your complete financial picture, not just this year's documents
  • Optimize: We identify opportunities for tax savings, including retirement contributions, charitable strategies, and timing of income and deductions
  • File: We prepare and e-file your return, ensuring accuracy and maximizing your refund or minimizing your liability
  • Plan: We provide year-round tax planning recommendations so you're never surprised at tax time

Year-Round Support

Your relationship with us doesn't end on April 15. We're available year-round for tax questions, estimated payment calculations, and proactive planning conversations.

body.processed:
Running a business is demanding enough without worrying about tax compliance and financial strategy. Our business advisory team serves as your outsourced CFO, providing the tax planning, compliance, and strategic guidance you need to grow profitably and confidently.

Business Tax Services

  • Corporate tax returns (C-Corp, S-Corp, Partnership, LLC)
  • Quarterly estimated tax calculations and filings
  • Sales tax compliance and nexus analysis
  • Payroll tax preparation and compliance
  • Business entity selection and restructuring
  • Tax credits (R&D credit, hiring credits, energy credits)

Advisory & Consulting

  • Financial statement preparation and analysis
  • Cash flow forecasting and management
  • Budgeting and profitability analysis
  • Business valuation
  • Mergers, acquisitions, and exit planning
  • Succession planning for family businesses

Industries We Serve

We have deep expertise in several industries, including medical and dental practices, real estate investment, construction, restaurants and hospitality, professional services firms, and technology startups. This specialized knowledge allows us to provide industry-specific insights and identify tax-saving opportunities unique to your field.

body.processed:
Accurate, up-to-date financial records are the foundation of good business decisions. Our bookkeeping and payroll team takes this essential but time-consuming work off your plate, so you can focus on running your business.

Bookkeeping Services

  • Monthly reconciliation of all bank and credit card accounts
  • Accounts payable and accounts receivable management
  • Financial statement preparation (income statement, balance sheet, cash flow)
  • Chart of accounts setup and optimization
  • QuickBooks, Xero, and FreshBooks setup, training, and support
  • 1099 preparation and filing

Payroll Services

  • Payroll processing (weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, or monthly)
  • Direct deposit setup and management
  • Federal, state, and local payroll tax filings
  • W-2 and 1099 preparation and distribution
  • Workers' compensation reporting
  • New hire reporting and onboarding paperwork

Technology-Forward Approach

We leverage cloud-based accounting platforms to give you real-time access to your financial data from anywhere. Our team integrates with the tools you already use, including your point-of-sale system, e-commerce platform, and banking feeds, creating a seamless, efficient workflow.

body.processed:
William Harrison co-founded Ledger & Associates in 1995 with a vision of building an accounting firm that treats every client with the same level of care and expertise, regardless of the size of their business or the complexity of their return. As Managing Partner, he sets the firm's strategic direction while maintaining an active client practice focused on business advisory and tax planning for closely held businesses.

With over 35 years of experience, William has guided hundreds of business owners through critical financial decisions, from startup financing to succession planning and business sales. He is known for his ability to translate complex tax and financial concepts into plain language, empowering his clients to make informed decisions with confidence.

William is active in the local business community, serving on the boards of the Chamber of Commerce and the Small Business Development Center. He is a frequent speaker on tax planning and business strategy at industry events.

body.processed:
Elena Ramirez is a Partner at Ledger & Associates and the firm's individual tax practice leader. With over 20 years of experience in tax preparation and planning, she specializes in complex individual returns, including those involving investment portfolios, real estate holdings, stock compensation, and multi-state filings.

Elena is an Enrolled Agent, authorized to represent taxpayers before the Internal Revenue Service. She has successfully resolved hundreds of IRS audits, notices, and collections matters, earning a reputation for her thoroughness and her calm, reassuring approach to what can be a stressful process.

She is passionate about financial literacy and volunteers her time leading free tax preparation workshops for underserved communities through the VITA (Volunteer Income Tax Assistance) program.

body.processed:
David Chang is a Senior CPA at Ledger & Associates with specialized expertise in audit, assurance, and forensic accounting. A Certified Fraud Examiner, he brings a meticulous analytical eye to every engagement, helping businesses identify financial risks, strengthen internal controls, and maintain regulatory compliance.

David leads the firm's audit and assurance practice, serving nonprofit organizations, employee benefit plans, and government-funded entities that require independent audits. He is also the go-to advisor for clients navigating complex regulatory requirements, including Sarbanes-Oxley compliance and nonprofit reporting standards.

Before joining Ledger & Associates, David spent seven years at a Big Four accounting firm, where he audited publicly traded companies in the technology and financial services sectors. He brings that institutional rigor to his work with the firm's clients.

body.processed:
The last quarter of the year is your final opportunity to take action that can meaningfully reduce your tax liability. Here are the most impactful strategies to consider before December 31.

Maximize Retirement Contributions

Contributing the maximum to tax-advantaged retirement accounts is one of the most effective tax-saving strategies available. For 2026, contribution limits are:

  • 401(k): $23,500 ($31,000 if age 50 or older)
  • Traditional or Roth IRA: $7,000 ($8,000 if age 50 or older)
  • SEP-IRA: Up to 25% of net self-employment income, maximum $69,000
  • Solo 401(k): Up to $69,000 ($76,500 if age 50 or older)

Harvest Tax Losses

Review your investment portfolio for positions with unrealized losses. Selling these investments before year-end allows you to offset capital gains dollar for dollar, and up to $3,000 in ordinary income. Be aware of the wash-sale rule, which prohibits repurchasing substantially identical securities within 30 days.

Bunch Charitable Donations

If your itemized deductions are close to the standard deduction threshold, consider 'bunching' two years' worth of charitable contributions into a single year to exceed the standard deduction. Donor-advised funds make this strategy especially convenient.

Review Business Expenses

If you own a business, consider accelerating deductible expenses into the current year. Purchase needed equipment, pay January invoices early, or prepay deductible expenses like insurance premiums or professional subscriptions.

Plan for Required Minimum Distributions

If you're age 73 or older, ensure you've taken your required minimum distribution (RMD) from traditional IRAs and employer retirement plans. The penalty for missing an RMD is 25% of the amount not withdrawn.
body.summary: The last quarter of the year is your final opportunity to take action that can meaningfully reduce your tax liability. Here are the most impactful strategies to consider before December 31.Maximize Re

image.alt: Financial planning documents and calendar showing year-end

summary.processed: Essential tax-saving moves to make before December 31, including retirement contributions, loss harvesting, and charitable strategies.
summary.summary: Essential tax-saving moves to make before December 31, including retirement contributions, loss harvesting, and charitable strategies.

entity.title: Starting a Business: Your Financial Checklist

body.processed:
Launching a new business is exciting, but getting your financial foundation right from the start will save you headaches, money, and potential legal problems down the road. Here's the essential financial checklist every new business owner should follow.

1. Choose the Right Business Entity

Your choice of entity -- sole proprietorship, LLC, S-Corporation, C-Corporation, or partnership -- has significant implications for taxes, liability protection, and how you pay yourself. Consult with a CPA before making this decision, as changing your entity later can be costly and complex.

2. Get an EIN

Apply for an Employer Identification Number (EIN) from the IRS. This is your business's federal tax ID, required for opening a bank account, hiring employees, and filing tax returns. It's free and can be obtained instantly online.

3. Separate Personal and Business Finances

Open a dedicated business checking account and credit card. Never comingle personal and business funds. This is essential for accurate bookkeeping, tax compliance, and maintaining your liability protection.

4. Set Up Accounting Software

Choose a cloud-based accounting platform (QuickBooks Online, Xero, or FreshBooks) and set it up correctly from day one. Connect your bank feeds, establish your chart of accounts, and learn how to categorize transactions.

5. Understand Your Tax Obligations

As a business owner, you're responsible for:

  • Quarterly estimated tax payments (federal and state)
  • Self-employment tax (15.3% on net earnings)
  • Sales tax collection and remittance (if applicable)
  • Payroll taxes (if you have employees)
  • Annual business tax returns

6. Keep Impeccable Records

Save all receipts and financial documents. The IRS can audit your business returns for three to six years after filing. Maintain organized records of income, expenses, mileage, home office use, and any other deductible business costs.

7. Build an Emergency Fund

Set aside three to six months of operating expenses in a separate savings account. Cash flow challenges are the number one reason small businesses fail. Having a financial cushion gives you the runway to weather slow periods.
body.summary: Launching a new business is exciting, but getting your financial foundation right from the start will save you headaches, money, and potential legal problems down the road. Here's the essential financ

summary.processed: The essential financial steps every new business owner must take, from choosing an entity to setting up accounting and understanding tax obligations.
summary.summary: The essential financial steps every new business owner must take, from choosing an entity to setting up accounting and understanding tax obligations.

entity.title: Retirement Planning at Every Age: A Decade-by-Decade Guide

body.processed:
Retirement planning is a lifelong process, not a one-time event. The strategies that make the most sense for you depend heavily on where you are in life. Here's what to focus on in each decade.

Your 20s: Start Early, Start Small

  • Enroll in your employer's 401(k) and contribute at least enough to get the full company match (that's free money)
  • Open a Roth IRA for tax-free growth -- you'll thank yourself decades from now
  • Build an emergency fund of 3-6 months of expenses before investing beyond retirement accounts
  • Avoid high-interest debt; if you have student loans, create a repayment strategy

Your 30s: Build Momentum

  • Increase your retirement contributions each time you get a raise
  • Diversify your investments across domestic and international stocks, bonds, and real estate
  • Purchase adequate life and disability insurance if you have a family
  • Begin saving for your children's education (529 plans offer tax advantages)

Your 40s: Maximize and Protect

  • Aim to have 3x your annual salary saved for retirement by age 40
  • Take advantage of catch-up contribution limits if you're behind
  • Review and update your estate plan (will, powers of attorney, beneficiary designations)
  • Pay off high-interest debt and avoid lifestyle inflation

Your 50s: Catch-Up and Refine

  • Make catch-up contributions to 401(k) ($7,500 additional) and IRA ($1,000 additional)
  • Start modeling your retirement income needs and Social Security benefits
  • Consider long-term care insurance
  • Begin planning for healthcare costs in retirement (consider HSA contributions)

Your 60s and Beyond: Transition and Distribute

  • Develop a detailed retirement income plan and withdrawal strategy
  • Decide when to claim Social Security (delaying to age 70 maximizes benefits)
  • Plan for Required Minimum Distributions beginning at age 73
  • Review your investment allocation for an appropriate mix of growth and income
  • Update your estate plan and beneficiary designations
body.summary: Retirement planning is a lifelong process, not a one-time event. The strategies that make the most sense for you depend heavily on where you are in life. Here's what to focus on in each decade.Your 20

image.alt: Couple reviewing retirement planning documents together

summary.processed: A practical guide to retirement planning priorities in your 20s, 30s, 40s, 50s, and beyond -- no matter where you're starting.
summary.summary: A practical guide to retirement planning priorities in your 20s, 30s, 40s, 50s, and beyond -- no matter where you're starting.

body.processed:
Our Firm

Ledger & Associates CPAs was founded in 1995 by William Harrison and Sandra Ledger with a simple mission: to provide small and mid-size businesses with the same caliber of accounting, tax, and advisory services traditionally reserved for large corporations. Three decades later, that mission continues to drive everything we do.

Our Approach

We believe that accounting is fundamentally a relationship business. Our clients don't just get a CPA -- they get a trusted advisor who understands their business, anticipates their needs, and proactively identifies opportunities to save money and reduce risk. We invest time in understanding each client's unique circumstances because that's where the real value lies.

Our Commitment to Technology

We embrace technology that makes our clients' lives easier. From secure online document sharing and electronic signatures to cloud-based accounting platforms and real-time financial dashboards, we use the latest tools to deliver faster, more efficient service without sacrificing the personal touch.

Community Involvement

Giving back is part of our culture. Our team volunteers with the VITA free tax preparation program, serves on the boards of local nonprofits, and sponsors financial literacy workshops for students and small business owners. We believe that a financially literate community is a stronger community.

Get in Touch

Whether you're a prospective client exploring your options or an existing client with a question, we'd love to hear from you. Our team is responsive, approachable, and always happy to help.

Office Location

Ledger & Associates CPAs
750 Commerce Drive, Suite 200
Maplewood, NJ 07040

Contact Information

  • Phone: (555) 678-9012
  • Fax: (555) 678-9013
  • Email: info@ledgerassociates.com

Office Hours

  • Monday - Friday: 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
  • Tax Season (February - April 15): Monday - Saturday, 8:00 AM - 7:00 PM
  • Saturday & Sunday: By appointment

Complimentary Consultation

We offer a free, no-obligation initial consultation for prospective clients. During this meeting, we'll discuss your financial needs, answer your questions, and explain how Ledger & Associates can help. Schedule your consultation by phone or using the form below.

Secure Document Upload

Need to send us tax documents or financial records? Use our secure client portal for encrypted, HIPAA-compliant document sharing. Existing clients can access the portal through the link in the top navigation.
